title: Cannot delete non-indexed columns on a table having any index
description:
### What happens?

- https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/issues/6737

but for `drop column`.

### To Reproduce

```sql
create table test (foo int, bar int);
create unique index foo_idx on test(foo);
alter table test drop column bar;
```

```
Dependency Error: Cannot alter entry "test" because there are entries that depend on it.
```

### OS:

Linux

### DuckDB Version:

v1.1.2 f680b7d08f

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Dave Tapley

### Affiliation:

JE Fuller

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have not tested with any build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have
poc: create table test (foo int, bar int);
alter table test drop column bar;
ALTER TABLE people ALTER name TYPE varchar(100);
key_bug_features: Cannot drop non-indexed columns with existing indexes
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test (foo int, bar int, baz varchar(50));
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX foo_idx ON test(foo);
INSERT INTO test (foo, bar, baz) VALUES (1, 2, 'example');
ALTER TABLE test DROP COLUMN bar;
CREATE TABLE orders (order_id int, order_date date, customer_id int, FOREIGN KEY(customer_id) REFERENCES test(foo));
